title: eriophyllum lanatum var. integrifolium
intro: Eriophyllum lanatum var. integrifolium, also known as woolly sunflower or Oregon sunshine, is a perennial herb native to the western United States and Canada. It is a member of the daisy family, characterized by its yellow flowers and woolly, silvery-gray foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing stress, and promoting overall well-being.
